sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the images, after the RIGHT action:
- Step 196 (before): The black F was between the charcoal and yellow F-shapes
- Step 197 (after): The black F has moved to the right, closer to the yellow F
The board changed, so RIGHT does move the player. Let me check the segmentation to see exactly where things are now.
I need to continue moving RIGHT to get closer to the yellow F. The goal seems to be to move the player (black F) to overlap or touch the yellow F.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: The RIGHT action moved the black F to the right. Let me check positions and continue moving toward the yellow F.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:  # Filter for significant objects
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min_r}-{max_r}, cols={min_c}-{max_c}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
